name: shadcn/ui
teaches: forms, tables, and dashboard building blocks
fast iteration patterns for real apps
avoid: treating copied components as a finished design direction
mixing too many unrelated variants just because they exist
why_this_matters: One of the fastest ways to learn how modern premium app UIs are assembled in practice.
reusable_lesson: Strong product UI usually comes from disciplined composition more than fancy decoration.

name: Radix Primitives
teaches: composable primitives for serious product UX
how strong accessibility infrastructure supports premium UI
avoid: assuming headless primitives provide a visual direction by themselves
overcomplicating simple product needs with unnecessary abstraction
why_this_matters: A strong way to learn the interaction discipline behind polished interfaces.
reusable_lesson: Premium-feeling UI still needs rigorous keyboard, focus, and state behavior underneath.

name: Mantine
focus: rapid product UI with good defaults
teaches: how to move fast without turning the UI into chaos
avoid: letting default components define the whole product personality
using convenience as an excuse for weak hierarchy
why_this_matters: Useful for learning how a productive design system stays coherent across many components.
reusable_lesson: Fast shipping is compatible with good frontend structure when the system is disciplined.

name: Chakra UI
focus: composable design tokens and layout primitives
avoid: building everything from generic boxes with no visual point of view
confusing flexible primitives with finished product design
why_this_matters: Helpful for learning how token systems and component ergonomics fit together.
reusable_lesson: A flexible system is strongest when layout discipline stays tighter than the API surface.

name: Primer React
avoid: blindly importing enterprise heaviness into lightweight products
why_this_matters: A strong source for learning product-system maturity and operational clarity.
reusable_lesson: Dense interfaces can still feel premium when hierarchy and consistency are tight.

name: Carbon Design System
teaches: how a design system scales across teams
avoid: bringing enterprise weight into simple landing pages
assuming more components always means better product UX
why_this_matters: Useful for learning system rigor and large-scale frontend discipline.
reusable_lesson: System quality comes from governance and consistency, not sheer component count.

name: Material UI
teaches: how a mature system handles customization
avoid: leaving the UI looking like untouched defaults
using design-system breadth as a substitute for product judgment
why_this_matters: A useful reference for large component coverage, theming depth, and admin-heavy interfaces.
reusable_lesson: A broad system is only premium when the product team shapes it intentionally.

name: Vercel AI Chatbot
teaches: how AI features fit into a serious product shell
avoid: mistaking AI chat chrome for the whole product experience
copying AI UI tropes without grounding them in user workflow
why_this_matters: A practical source for modern AI interaction patterns that feel productized.
reusable_lesson: AI interfaces feel stronger when chat is integrated into a real workflow, not floating by itself.

name: assistant-ui
focus: AI chat components and tool-call UX
teaches: how to make AI interactions feel structured instead of messy
avoid: over-designing chat chrome while neglecting the surrounding workflow
assuming a chat UI library defines the full product strategy
why_this_matters: Useful for learning the mechanics of serious AI chat interfaces and tool interaction patterns.
reusable_lesson: AI UX becomes more credible when messages, actions, and tool output share one consistent system.
